front: Film: the inventor Rotwang builds a 'machine human' — a robot — in the likeness of the underground labor leader Maria
Film: the protagonist Freder hallucinates workers pulling levers resembling clock faces being fed to the machine-god Moloch
Film: Eugen Schufftan invented his process of combining miniatures and mirrors for this film's special effects
Film: lost reels of this 1927 silent film were rediscovered in Buenos Aires in 2008
Film: Heinz Schulz-Neudamm's poster for this film is the most expensive film poster ever sold; Boris Balinsky's poster was inspired by Piranesi
Film: the tallest building in the city is the Bruegel-inspired New Tower of Babel; sometimes screened with a score by Giorgio Moroder
Painting: a triptych whose central panel shows society women dancing the Charleston to a jazz band at a Weimar Germany ball, with Alfred Schulze on saxophone
Painting: the outer panels of this triptych show crippled WWI veterans on wooden peg legs begging and being ignored by prostitutes in fur coats
Painting: the artist included a self-portrait on the left panel as a wounded veteran with two wooden peg legs, attacked by a dog — also explored in his The Trench and the War series
Painting: figures include a hefty blonde woman with her right breast exposed through a pink coat clutching cash, and a man in dress and pearls waving a pink ostrich feather
